title: Movies Death Deathtrue Deathtrue
wikitext:
[]: Movies [Death, Death(true), Death(true)²]             Quote Post by samcsli » Fri Nov 26, 2010 9:40 pm movie1997-03-15《Death》&《Rebirth》 movie1997-07-19《THE END OF EVANGELION》=《Air》&《I need you》 tv 1998-01-02《Death(true) & Rebirth》 movie1998-03-07《REVIVAL OF EVANGELION》=《Death(true)2》《Air》&《I need you》

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Nov 26, 2010 9:51 pm 1. Under normal circumstances we can't give (and ask) download links anyway I'm not sure if there are video releases pre-Death True^2. 2. Death True ^2 has the "this is how Evangelion Death was meant to be done" message, I don't know if there was a similar message for Death True but surely that text is lacking in the original Death. 3. As far as I know no, and actually there's only one Rebirth, Death True^2 is followed directly by "Air/Magokoro wo kimi ni" in the Renewal (I'm not sure about Death True). EoE has been changed only in the original video release but Air should be the same, it's Magokoro wo kimi ni which has been changed (more infos here ). Here's a list of differences between Rebirth and Air (but unless I misunderstood you it wasn't what you were asking): http://wiki.evageeks.org/Guides:Rebirth_vs._Episode_25%27

[]:             Quote Post by samcsli » Sat Nov 27, 2010 3:39 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: 1. Under normal circumstances we can't give (and ask) download links anyway I'm not sure if there are video releases pre-Death True^2....

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Nov 27, 2010 5:04 am "<Search> bakabt" Type that into google. I found Death True^2 that way. Oh and by the way, it´s usually against the rules to download from the BakaBT tracker without an account, but one movie won´t hurt them ;)

[]:             Quote Post by psychosis090 » Sat Nov 27, 2010 6:31 am I hope this isn't too off-topic, but has anyone ever made and/or distributed subtitles for End of Evangelion using Bochan Bird's translation?

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Sat Nov 27, 2010 10:07 am Offtopic: More likely, but one enterprising group (I won't say who are they or where it came from) subbed EoE several years ago. Furthermore: http://www.opensubtitles.org/en/search/sublanguageid-all/idmovie-501 http://subscene.com/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-The-End-of-Evangelion-Shin-seiki-Evangelion-Gekij-ban-AirMagokoro-wo-kimi-ni/subtitles-79738.aspx With these, however, your quality mileage may vary. Ontopic: watching the "Death" movies is optional (unless one is a completionist), as the first half is more of a summary of the events in the TV show, and the second half is the first installment of EoE. However, EoE supplants and exceeds "Death" in terms of quality and (I think) continuity.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sat Nov 27, 2010 12:23 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote: "<Search> bakabt" Type that into google. I found Death True^2 that way. Oh and by the way, it´s usually against the rules to download from the BakaBT tracker without an account, but one movie won´t hurt them ;)

[]:             Quote Post by samcsli » Sat Nov 27, 2010 5:10 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: samcsli is asking for Death (the first theatrical edition) and Death True (not True^2) which was the version edited for the satellite channel Wowow. Actually I've downloaded many things from bakabt without using an account and it doesn't seem that they never cared to ban me (even though it'd be useless, I've a dynamic address). According to the rules we're supposed to don't ask/give links tout a court; the main risk is that it turns in a board with many similar discussions and we could get the attention of some undesirable fellows. The scenes that were cutted from Death are present in the DC of the TV series (someone could say that they removed to force people to buy both of them but I'm not sure if it's the case), Death True^2 reinserted many of them (like Gendo's Adam) but there are always missing scenes. Are you referring to the wiki page? As I said it's a comparison between Evangelion:REBIRTH and Evangelion Episode 25' Air.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Sat Nov 27, 2010 6:32 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Actually I've downloaded many things from bakabt without using an account and it doesn't seem that they never cared to ban me

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Mon Dec 06, 2010 8:52 pm On tangent- why in the world is there no good rip of the original death? Where is the R2 source? Is there a DVD set that has it? Just VHS? Stuff like this is falling through the cracks of history, just like Arianne.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Dec 06, 2010 9:12 pm Clover wrote: On tangent- why in the world is there no good rip of the original death? Where is the R2 source? Is there a DVD set that has it? Just VHS? Stuff like this is falling through the cracks of history, just like Arianne.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Tue Dec 07, 2010 3:02 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Can you even confirm that Death was included on home release? Death(true) could have been recorded off of WoWoW, so with that it's a matter of finding someone with a VHS copy of it.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Wed Dec 08, 2010 2:08 am The only proper version of Rebirth on DVD was released in Japan back in 1999. It was part of a 2-disc set. Disc 1 contained "Death (True)²" + "Rebirth"; Disc 2 had "Air" and "Magokoro wo, Kimi ni." Both discs had letterboxed video within a 4:3 standard frame (i.e. NOT anamorphic 16:9 like the Renewal version.) Rebirth didn't make it over to the latter Renewal movie releases (KIBA-1009 [Dolby] and KIBA-1209 [DTS]) so this is the only way to get it. Catalog: KIBA-105 UPC/EAN: 4988003932664 In case you're wondering, the Manga R1 release of "Death and Rebirth" used the audio + video from "Air" and copy/pasted the appropriate telop screens from the actual "Rebirth" where necessary. It's not the same as "Rebirth" in terms of seeing the differences in audio, video, and cuts used.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Dec 08, 2010 1:29 pm @svenge: So this is the only DVD of the real rebirth in existence? You wouldn't happen to own this would you? Does anyone on this site own it? http://images.jungle.homelinux.org/?601590,l

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Wed Dec 08, 2010 1:48 pm That's the one. Sadly, I don't own it myself (it's the one pre-Renewal R2J DVD I'm missing.)

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Dec 08, 2010 3:35 pm Clover wrote: @svenge: So this is the only DVD of the real rebirth in existence? You wouldn't happen to own this would you? Does anyone on this site own it? http://images.jungle.homelinux.org/?601590,l

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Dec 08, 2010 5:44 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I think I can manage to track down a copy of that. I got this.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Wed Dec 08, 2010 5:58 pm Yeah, I agree. The 2 "holy grails" of Evangelion video are: 1) The "Genesis" version of End of Evangelion (LD Vols. 13+14; also on DVD catalog no. KIBA-7) 2) The proper "Rebirth" part of "Death and Rebirth". (LD Movie Box Set; also on DVD catalog no. KIBA-105) Everything else is found relatively easily in retail, eBay, or DVDISO trackers, let alone MKV video rips.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Dec 08, 2010 6:45 pm What we need to do is find a hardcore film collector that collects film prints of anime films. They're out there. I know they are. There has to be a surviving film print of Death & Rebirth. The challenge is finding it.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Dec 08, 2010 7:55 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: What we need to do is find a hardcore film collector that collects film prints of anime films. They're out there. I know they are. There has to be a surviving film print of Death & Rebirth. The challenge is finding it.
